title: dianthus plumarius subsp. blandus
intro: Dianthus plumarius subsp. blandus, also known as the fragrant pink pink-flowered wild pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dianthus plumarius subsp. blandus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing its flowers in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and topical creams, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
